stem: A banking study surveys 25 approved loans and observes 9 positive outcomes out of 25 (36.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (12 observations, rate = 33.3%) and Group B (13 observations, rate = 38.5%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 33% and 47% of the population. Estimate the true loan default rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.36. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.366 (range [0.3627, 0.3692]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.2025, 0.5548]. Point estimate: 0.366.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.4: 0.4×0.338 + 0.6×0.3846 = 0.366.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=25, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
